article_meta.title: Federal immigration officers board an elevator with respondents at immigration court in New York on 17 November 2025

reasoning: The summary adopts some of the article’s loaded framing (e.g., “spied,” asserting a broader pattern of “criminalizing” activism) without consistent attribution, and omits key countervailing perspectives present in the source. This yields noticeable slant and framing inheritance.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses loaded verbs/phrases like “spied” and “criminalizing” in the model’s voice; some critical language is attributed but not all.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Most claims are supported by the article; the “broader pattern” assertion lacks explicit attribution/evidence in the summary.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: States the case “fits a broader pattern” in its own voice; treats contested framing as fact.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Language is somewhat charged but not highly alarmist; largely mirrors the article’s tone.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks by the model.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about unobservable mental states.
custom_reasoning.slant: Emphasizes critics and civil liberties framing; downplays/omits law enforcement and DOJ perspectives included in the article.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Excludes NYPD statement/context, DOJ spokesperson’s comments, and FBI/NYPD perspectives; “sources_represented” lists only The Guardian.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Includes evaluative phrasing like “overreach,” “threat,” and “broader pattern,” though some is attributed.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically loaded terms (“spied,” “criminalizing”) used where neutral alternatives exist.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Focuses on negative implications; limited inclusion of other context from law enforcement.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Leans toward activist/civil liberties viewpoints over law enforcement/DOJ but not extreme.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Retains the source’s framing instead of consistently neutral paraphrase.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Misses major perspectives quoted in the article (law enforcement statements, DOJ response).
